name: verify-endpoint-params-against-code-not-memory
tags: memory staleness, endpoint params, verify before trusting, api params, session endpoint, task:61703ef7
body:
A memory or docstring claiming an API endpoint accepts a specific query param (e.g. "?cwd=<path>") should be verified against the actual route handler before relying on it — an unrecognized query param to a FastAPI/Starlette route is silently ignored by default, not rejected, so a wrong claim about accepted params doesn't fail loudly; it just quietly does nothing.

Why: memory verify-session-id-via-hooks-server claimed GET /session accepted an optional ?cwd=<path> filter. The actual hooks/server.py handler takes no params at all and returns every session unfiltered. The mistake persisted because passing the bogus param never errored.

How to apply: before trusting a memory's claim about an endpoint's accepted parameters, grep the route handler's function signature directly — don't assume a memory is current just because it reads confidently.
